article_title: Airborne Electromagnetic Survey Reveals a Freshwater Reservoir Up to Four Kilometers Deep Beneath the Great Salt Lake

content_preview.title: Airborne Electromagnetic Survey Reveals a Freshwater Reservoir Up to Four Kilometers Deep Beneath the Great Salt Lake
content_preview.summary: University of Utah geophysicists have discovered a vast freshwater system saturating sediments beneath the shrinking Great Salt Lake, potentially spanning its entire 1,500-square-mile footprint.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team of University of Utah geophysicists has discovered an extensive freshwater reservoir hidden beneath the Great Salt Lake, one of the most saline bodies of water in the Western Hemisphere. Using airborne electromagnetic surveys flown by helicopter over Farmington Bay, the researchers found that freshwater saturates the sediments beneath the lake's hypersaline surface to depths of three to four kilometers, far exceeding expectations and raising the possibility that the reservoir...
